rollup.notes: Under ceiling; 2 Towne hotels + Capital Grille hometown-reclassed; Y2 Elite M&A (5 accts)

meta.methodology: v3 (April 2026) — penetration engine with conservative Y5 caps: 50% Charlotte/Indianapolis, 30% other markets. S-curve scaled to caps. Anchor (SOPHI-already) accounts forced Y1; v7 accounts forced Y1/Y2; both count toward cap. Per-operator credibility gates, cumulative ownership-group multiplier, binary acquisition with full-TAM accrual.

sensitivity.scenario: Base case — all 6 markets, Indy v7 w/ M&A
Stress — Cold markets only (no Charlotte, no Indy)
Indianapolis deep-dive: organic vs M&A split

lp_headlines.note: $ - sum of Conservative TAM across 6 markets (locked per-market)
38.1% of TAM — after 4 structural filters + 50% ceiling

lp_headlines.metric: • 6-market portfolio spans 1 warm market (Charlotte, 4 SOPHI anchors) and 5 cold markets (Phoenix, Cleveland, Louisville, Denver, Indianapolis).
• SAM structurally bounded: 50% TAM ceiling enforced in Charlotte + Denver; other 4 markets land under ceiling naturally (11.5-40%).
• Denver is the anchor market — 32% of portfolio TAM, 42% of portfolio Y5 SOM.

• Indianapolis v7 adds two differentiators: (1) hometown-advantage Towne Park displacement at lower-volume hotels (Omni, Conrad) + Capital Grille, (2) Y2 Elite Management acquisition absorbing 5 restaurant contracts at 100% TAM capture.

• Qualitative upside (not modeled): Enterprise lock-ups (top-5 national operators, ~$50M TAM portfolio-wide), Partnership pool (bundled mgmt contracts, ~$10M TAM), healthcare/airport/venue vertical ($0 TAM by methodology).
• Cold S-curve: 40/50/70/85/95 over 5 years. Warm S-curve: 50/65/80/90/95. Capacity ramp 6/14/23/33/43.

meta.conservative_caveat: Penetration is intentionally capped below operational signal. Uplifts NOT modeled: relationship multiplier compounding beyond first-tier sister properties, occupancy improvements vs Sophi operational data, valet-conversion lift from Sophi vs status-quo operators, brand-flag network effects, post-Y5 continued penetration. Material upside left on the table for fundraise defensibility.
